title: Retail in 2026: Growth, competition, and the case for consumption ...
snippet: India’s retail story continues to unfold across two parallel markets. Urban India is seeing faster adoption of organised formats, brand-led consumption, and digitally enabled shopping journeys. Bharat remains anchored in neighbourhood stores and trusted local retailers, where relationships, affordability, and local relevance matter more than scale. title: How India Shops Online 2025 | Bain & Company
snippet:
In India, hyper-value commerce’s contribution to e-retail has scaled from around 5% of e-retail GMV in 2021 to more than 12% in 2024. These platforms have gained strong traction among lower-middle-income consumers, particularly in Tier-2 or smaller cities, by focusing on an affordable product assortment. Additionally, these platforms have rapidly expanded their seller base by offering seller financing and zero-commission models.

title: India's retail market: growth, lifestyle consumption & new markets
snippet:
# Online growth, lifestyle spending, and new markets are driving retail in India

19.03.2026

India is coming increasingly into focus in international retail strategies – and for good reason. The market is not only growing at an impressive pace, it is also changing structurally at the same time. New consumer groups, rising purchasing power, and digital infrastructure are strengthening retail. At the same time, regional differences, a young, trend

snippet:
India remains one of the world's fastest-growing economies, with a GDP growth rate of 8 per cent in 2025. This economic strength is fueling a massive consumption momentum that puts the country on track to become the third-largest economy globally by 2030.
